summary: A complex industrial or utility structure featuring two tall stonebrick towers with overhanging roofs, connected to a lower rectangular building with horizontal striped walls. The main structure includes a central tower (taller), a secondary tower, and a brick chimney-like element on the left. White fence posts outline the perimeter. The building sits on a brown dirt foundation with a wooden interior framework visible.
building_type: Industrial/Utility Structure (possibly factory, mill, or processing facility)

shape.overall_form: Asymmetrical complex with two prominent vertical towers and a lower horizontal wing
shape.symmetry: Asymmetrical; towers are offset, main building extends to the right

materials.name: wood
materials.role: horizontal striped wall panels and roof overhangs

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof caps and overhangs on towers

elements: two tall towers with mushroom-cap roofs
rebuild_hints: Build two tall stonebrick towers of different heights (main tower ~12 blocks, secondary ~10 blocks)
Add flat stone slab roofs with overhanging edges to both towers
Construct lower rectangular building with alternating wood and stone horizontal stripes
Place brick chimney structure on left side with protruding wooden beams
Outline entire structure with white fence posts at ground level
Add small window openings to tower faces
Use wood planks for horizontal striping pattern on main building walls
Ensure roof overhangs extend 2-3 blocks beyond tower walls
Place wooden interior framework visible through walls
uncertainties: Exact height of secondary tower relative to main tower (appears 1-2 blocks shorter)
Precise number of horizontal stripes on main building (appears 4-5 layers)
Internal structure details not fully visible from exterior views
Exact window placement and size on towers
Whether the brick element is a chimney or decorative accent
